raw_text:
Nexus Platform Labs

Empowering engineers through intelligent internal developer platforms

Now Hiring: Director of Platform Engineering

San Francisco, CA (Remote-First) · Full-Time · $320,000 - $420,000 per year

W

e are seeking an experienced Director of Platform Engineering to lead our platform infrastructure team

and drive the technical vision for our internal developer platform. In this role, you will oversee 2-3

engineering managers, multiple teams (~25 engineers), and be responsible for the architecture, reliability, and

evolution of our core platform services. You will work closely with our VP of Engineering and Product

leadership to define roadmaps, establish engineering excellence standards, and mentor senior engineers. This is
a strategic leadership position requiring deep technical expertise combined with organizational leadership

experience.

A B O U T U S

Nexus Platform Labs is a fast-growing infrastructure technology company focused on building next-generation

internal developer platforms (IDPs) that scale with modern organizations. We help enterprises automate their

development workflows, reduce cognitive load on engineers, and accelerate time-to-market through intelligent

platform abstractions and self-service capabilities. Founded in 2019, we've grown to serve over 200+ mid-

market and enterprise customers across fintech, healthcare, and SaaS industries.

Y O U R R O L E

Lead and develop 2-3 engineering managers, conducting annual reviews, career development planning, and

succession planning for your organization

Set technical vision and architecture direction for platform services; make critical decisions on technology

choices (e.g., Kubernetes clusters, Terraform state management, policy-as-code frameworks)

Oversee the design, implementation, and maintenance of core platform components including Kubernetes

infrastructure, IDP tooling, and self-service developer portal built on Backstage

Drive organizational reliability and operational excellence by establishing SLOs, implementing

observability standards, and fostering a blameless incident response culture

Partner with Product and Customer Success to translate customer needs into platform roadmap priorities;
balance technical debt with feature velocity

Champion adoption of platform tooling (Terraform, ArgoCD, policy engines) across all engineering teams;

lead communities of practice and knowledge-sharing initiatives

Build and scale platform engineering hiring; define competency models and interview rubrics aligned with

the organization's growth plan

Establish metrics and KPIs for platform health; regularly report on team velocity, platform reliability

(SLOs), and developer experience metrics to executive leadership

 R E Q U I R E D Q U A L I F I C AT I O N S

10+ years of software engineering experience, with at least 5+ years in platform engineering, infrastructure,

or DevOps roles

3+ years of direct people management experience, including managing managers and building high-
performing cross-functional teams

Deep expertise with Kubernetes architecture, deployment patterns, and operational best practices in

production environments

Strong experience with Infrastructure-as-Code tools (Terraform, CloudFormation) and version-control-

driven infrastructure automation

Proficiency in at least one systems programming language (Go, Python, Rust) for building internal tooling

and platform services

Demonstrated ability to influence and drive technical decisions across multiple teams without direct

authority; strong stakeholder management skills

Track record of designing and implementing large-scale systems that impact 50+ engineers; experience
building internal platforms or shared services

D E S I R E D Q U A L I F I C AT I O N S

Hands-on experience with Backstage or other open-source internal developer portal solutions

Experience with policy-as-code frameworks (Kyverno, OPA/Rego) and security policy automation

Background in SRE or production systems engineering; experience defining and achieving SLOs

Familiarity with multi-cluster or multi-cloud Kubernetes architectures

Active involvement in engineering leadership communities or speaking at conferences on platform
engineering topics

T O O L S  O F  T H E T R A D E

Kubernetes, Terraform, Backstage, Go, Python, ArgoCD, Kyverno, Prometheus, Grafana, GitHub Actions

W E O F F E R

Competitive equity package (stock options) with a multi-year vesting schedule

Comprehensive health benefits including medical, dental, vision, and mental health coverage

Unlimited PTO policy with encouragement to take 25+ days per year

Professional development budget ($5,000 annually) for conferences, courses, and certifications

Home office setup allowance and annual equipment refresh budget

Parental leave (16 weeks for primary caregivers, 8 weeks for secondary)

Learning stipend for technical books, platforms, and training resources

Annual company offsites and team building events

 T O A P P LY

Please submit your resume, a cover letter highlighting your experience leading platform engineering teams, and

links to any public technical writing or conference talks (if available) to careers@nexusplatformlabs.com.

Include 'Director Platform Engineering' in the subject line. Applications reviewed on a rolling basis.
job_title: Empowering engineers through intelligent internal developer platforms
